description_long: This is a rounded, double-handled ceramic vase with a wide neck and a slightly flared rim. The surface is adorned with hand-painted geometric patterns in earthy tones, including bands of triangles, dots, and stylized motifs that may represent abstract faces or architectural forms. The handles are robust and symmetrically placed on either side, providing both functional grip and visual balance. The neck features a chevron pattern, while the body displays horizontal bands with repeating geometric shapes. The vase has a matte, slightly rough finish, typical of traditional pottery. Its coloration ranges from light tan to dark brown, with black and white painted accents.
description: A rounded, double-handled ceramic vase with geometric and abstract painted patterns in earthy tones and a matte finish.
description_view_0: Front view showing both handles, the wide neck with chevron pattern, and the main geometric motifs on the body.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting one handle and the vertical banded pattern along the neck, with geometric motifs wrapping around the body.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, showing both handles and the continuation of the geometric patterns, but with less visible face-like motif.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with one handle and vertical banded neck, displaying the geometric motifs from a different angle.
